auth_notes: Requires creating a Developer Application in the Clio Manage portal to obtain a Client ID and Client Secret. Supports Authorization Code Grant flow. Regional endpoints (US, EU, CA) must be specified in the token and authorization URLs.
gotchas: Regional base URLs differ (app.clio.com, eu.app.clio.com, ca.app.clio.com) and must be consistent across auth and requests.
Pagination uses a 'next' link in the metadata which requires manual loop configuration in n8n.
Strict rate limiting may require the use of n8n Wait nodes or Split In Batches for large data migrations.
client_must_provide: Clio Account Region (US, EU, or Canada)
Admin access to Clio Manage to create the Developer App
research_notes: The integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. OAuth2 credentials in n8n should be configured with the specific regional URLs (e.g., https://app.clio.com/oauth/authorize). Webhooks must be registered via a POST request to the /webhooks endpoint as there is no UI-based webhook management in Clio.

effort_recommendation.rationale: The integration will rely heavily on the HTTP Request node. OAuth2 credentials in n8n should be configured with the specific regional URLs (e.g., https://app.clio.com/oauth/authorize). Webhooks must be registered via a POST request to the /webhooks endpoint as there is no UI-based webhook management in Clio.
